prompt:
Our conference-center lobby display reads status records produced by panel.c from a local file; this is an offline formatting utility with no networking or access-control behavior. After last week's cleanup, three ordinary rendering regressions appeared. First, each generated record is one byte longer than the fixture produced by the display supplier's reference utility and begins with different bytes, so the display simulator rejects it. Second, a SERVICE record is labeled OVERRIDE. Third, REMOTE records are treated as though that label does not exist. The implementation still compiles cleanly under -Wall -Wextra -Werror, so this is a C literal/table debugging task rather than a compiler-error task.

Run make test. The tests compare the generated records byte-for-byte with the supplier's frozen reference fixtures and pin the complete label table. Find and correct the implementation defects. test_panel.c, Makefile, mintest.h, and .gitignore are fixed; do not modify them or the expected bytes.
notes: Authoring-time toolchain: gcc 16.1.1 (-std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror). Both planted defects (octal escape overrun \128, adjacent string-literal concatenation via missing comma) probed to compile warning-free on this box, so the baseline fails at run time, not build time.
